logistics.explanation: Self-supported format in Incheon allows flexibility for wild camping or commercial sites near Gangwon checkpoints. Start from Yangyang in Gangwondo requires planning for multi-day travel from Incheon Airport. Group start at 7 AM simplifies coordination for 1500 participants.

length.explanation: 62-mile distance suits a single-day Gran Fondo effort from Incheon paved roads. Fits within the shorter Epic Offroad 300 division's 325km total for multi-day context. Ideal for regional riders building to longer Korea Epic stages.

technicality.explanation: 100% paved roads around Incheon demand no offroad skills. Smooth surfaces from Gangwon start focus on endurance over handling. Beginner-friendly for road discipline in South Korea's Gran Fondo World Tour stop.

elevation.explanation: 2500 feet gain challenges with steady climbs near Incheon coastal terrain. Mirrors scaled-down version of Epic Road 1000's 18,500m over 1040km. Key for building climbing legs in June humidity.

climate.explanation: June weather in Incheon offers mild temperatures ideal for long paved efforts. Low rain risk enhances multi-day festival vibe from Gangwon base. Coastal breezes aid recovery post-62 miles.

altitude.explanation: Low elevations around Incheon sea level minimize hypoxia risks. Gangwon checkpoints stay below 1000m like full Epic courses. Focus remains on heat management over thin air.

adventure.explanation: Self-supported group ride from Incheon explores paved Korean roads with checkpoint freedom. Gran Fondo World Tour status adds festival energy without extreme bikepacking. Structured yet flexible for 62-mile adventure.

prestige.explanation: Gran Fondo World Tour stop elevates Korea Epic Ride's regional draw. Founded in 2018 with 1500 typical participants in Incheon area. Bikepacking format distinguishes it among South Korean road events.

race_quality.explanation: High-quality organization as multi-day festival in Incheon with e-bike allowance. Paved 62-mile course rivals Chuncheon Baehuryeong Fondo standards. Self-supported ethos ensures fair group ride experience.

experience.explanation: Immersive cultural ride through Incheon's paved landscapes and Gangwon vibes. Gran Fondo World Tour perks include picturesque checkpoints. Memorable for 2500ft gain in June festival setting.

community.explanation: 1500 participants foster strong group start camaraderie from Yangyang. Regional South Korean cyclists connect via shared checkpoints. Gran Fondo network boosts international community ties.

field_depth.explanation: Around 1500 entrants provide solid regional depth under 5000 threshold. Mix of road and bikepacking enthusiasts from Incheon events. Quality over quantity in self-supported format.

value.explanation: Affordable entry for 62-mile Gran Fondo with multi-day festival access in Incheon. Self-supported cuts costs on Gangwon accommodations. High return via World Tour prestige.

expenses.explanation: Low fees for paved road event near Incheon Airport minimize travel costs. Wild camping options slash Gangwon lodging expenses. Great value for 2500ft elevation challenge.

cultural_impact.explanation: Regional draw in South Korea's Incheon-Gangwon corridor promotes cycling tourism. Gran Fondo World Tour spot highlights local paved routes. Engages domestic community since 2018 founding.

course_description.summary: Korea Epic Ride offers a 62-mile paved road challenge in Incheon, South Korea, as part of a multi-day Gran Fondo World Tour cycling festival. Riders tackle approximately 2500 feet of elevation on smooth coastal and inland roads, passing key checkpoints in a self-supported format. The June event emphasizes endurance and scenic Korean landscapes from Incheon base.

key_segments.name: Incheon Coastal Climb
key_segments.description: Initial paved ascent along Incheon shores building early elevation.

key_segments.name: Gangwon Checkpoint Push
key_segments.description: Rolling roads toward Yangyang-style inland sections with steady gains.

key_segments.name: Final Incheon Descent
key_segments.description: Fast paved return with recovery rolling terrain.

course_description.elevation_profile: Steady climbs totaling 2500ft with multiple pitches mimicking scaled Epic Road division, peaking mid-course before Incheon descent.
course_description.aid_stations: Self-supported; riders source from roadside groceries or restaurants near Incheon and Gangwon checkpoints.

travel.accommodation: Wild camping, commercial campsites, or hotels in Incheon/Gangwon
travel.bike_transport: Fly to ICN; self-supported with personal bike

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 2000 miles/year with hill repeats.
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: 8-12 weeks building to 60+ mile rides with 2000-3000ft elevation.
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x 2-hour tempo rides; 1x 4-hour endurance with Incheon-style climbs; recovery spins.

history.notable_facts: Gran Fondo World Tour member since inception; self-supported bikepacking group ride format; e-bikes permitted from 2026.

final_verdict.one_liner: Scenic paved road fondo in Korea's cycling festival scene.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Road enthusiasts seeking Gran Fondo prestige with self-supported adventure.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Gravel purists or those avoiding humid June multi-day events.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Pack light for Incheon wild camping; hit Gangwon groceries early for resupply.

suffering_zones.label: Mid-Course Gangwon Grind
suffering_zones.description: 2500ft cumulative climbs peak here on paved pitches after Incheon flats.

suffering_zones.label: June Humidity Hammer
suffering_zones.description: Coastal moisture turns 62 miles into endurance test post-checkpoint.
